title: Codonanthe ventricosa
taxonomy: Codonanthe ventricosa belongs to the family Bignonaceae. It is a genus of flowering plants in the family Bignonaceae, native to South America. Codonanthe ventricosa is specifically classified within the order Lamiales. The plant is also known as a member of the subfamily Bignonioideae. Within the broader classification, it falls under the kingdom Plantae.

intro: Codonanthe ventricosa, also known as "monkey face" or "monkey face plant," is a species of flowering plant in the family Bignoniaceae. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its purported health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may help to alleviate symptoms of fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Codonanthe ventricosa include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Codonanthe ventricosa is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various ailments, including respiratory infections and gastrointestinal disorders.
